audit_items.proof_trace: Two unchecked malloc paths produce definite null pointer dereferences on OOM: (1) utbm_create_pattern L236-237 calls malloc then immediately passes the result to memset without a null check; (2) utbm_compile L289-296 stores malloc/realloc results into p->pat and p->skip without null checks, then L304 initializes cp=p->pat and L332/L356 write cp->uc unconditionally. The proof that the property holds fails at both allocation sites because no guard prevents the NULL dereference.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker triggers repeated LDAP substring search requests to exhaust server memory; when the LDAP server calls utbm_create_pattern or utbm_compile under OOM and malloc returns NULL, the subsequent memset or field write dereferences NULL, crashing the slapd process (DoS).
